Alternative is the same verb used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Kepa-k itsaso-a-ri begira-tzen d-i-o.” [Петя-erg море-sg-dat смотреть-ipfv abs3prs-erg/abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg[erg3sg]] ‘Петя смотрит на море’. Some speakers report a semantic nuance associated with the two patterns, so that ALL highlights the location meaning (direction of gaze), whereas DAT highlights the object itself. When Y is ‘clouds” the allative is more natural. A further alternative is “so egin” [gaze make]”.

Alternatives are 1) the verb “jelostu” used the TR or ABS_DAT pattern, “Kepa-k Miren jelos-ten d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша[abs] завидовать-imp abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]], 2) the verb “inbidiatu” used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Kepa-k Miren-i inbidia-tzen d-i-o.” [Петя-erg Маша-dat завидовать-imp abs3prs-erg/abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg[erg3sg]] and 3) several nominal constructions, such as “Kepak Mireni inbidia dio.” and “Kepak Mirenen inbidia du.”

Further alternatives are several complex verbs, typically alllowing the same two patterns: “borroka egin”, “borroka-n ibili”, “borroka ari izan”.

A further alternative is a CV “dei/ deiadar/ hots egin” [call make], which is used with the erg-dat pattern.

This is the most frequent pattern for this meaning. Near synonyms are the verbs “esetsi” and “jazarri” used in the same ERG_DAT pattern. Further alternatives include 1)  “oldartu” and “jazarri” used in the ABS_DAT pattern: “Hartz-a arrantzale-a-ri oldar-tu zai-o” [Медведь-sg[abs] рыбак-sg-dat напасть-pfv [abs3prs]abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg] ‘Медведь напал на рыбака’ and 2) the verb “jauki” used in the TR pattern (found in the French Basque Country). Apart from this, a possible alternative is the CV “eraso-a jo” [attack hit], used in the ERG_DAT pattern. Finally, the verb “jazarri” can also be used in the ABS_DAT pattern.
Near synonym is “erdi”, used in the same pattern. One more alternative is “seme-a izan” [son-abs[sg] have].

Same as 42. Same verb but different pattern as 82. Alternative is the DAT_ABS pattern, which emphasizes the lack of control on the part of the X argument: “Kepa-ri giltz-ak gal-du zai-zki-o.” [Петя-dat ключ-pl[abs] терять-pfv [abs3prs]abs/dat-abs.pl-dat3sg] ‘У Пети потерялись ключи’.
Same verb as in 42, 103, different pattern. This is basically a trivalent verb, where the absolutive slot is routinely occupied by words like “game” etc. But this slot can also be left unoccupied.
Alternative is the verb “ikuzi”, also used in the TR pattern.
A near synonym is “geratu”, used in the same DAT_ABS pattern.

CV. A near synonym is the CV “begi-ko izan” [eye[sg]-genl) have], also used in the TR pattern. The same CVs “begiko izan” and “gogoko izan” can also be used in the DAT_ABS pattern: "Kepa-ri Miren begi-ko zai-o." [Петя-dat Маша[abs] глаз[sg]-genl [abs3prs]abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg] ‘Петя симпатизирует Маше’.

Near synonyms are “oroitu”, “gomutatu”, “bururatu”, all used in the same TR pattern. The verbs “gogoratu”, “oroitu”, “gomutatu” can also be used in the ABS_INS pattern, recommended in some sources (Etxebarria 1995): “Kepa etxe-a-z ondo gogora-tzen da.” [Петя[abs] дом-sg-ins хорошо помнить-ipfv abs3sg.prs] ‘Петя хорошо помнит дом’. Apart from this, all these verbs can also be used in the DAT_ABS pattern, in which case they mean ‘come to mind’.
Alternative is “irauli” used in the same TR pattern.

Near synonyms are “higuindu”, “gaitzetsi”, “herratu”, all used in the TR pattern. Further possibilities include CVs “hastio izan” [hatred have] used in the TR pattern, also “gorroto izan” [hatred have] used in either the TR pattern of the ERG_DAT pattern and also “aiher izan” [hatred be] used in the DAT_ABS pattern.
Same as 119. There are many alternative ways to convey the same meaning. The verb “gustatu” is sometimews used in a different pattern.

Same as 58. Alternatives are the CV “maite izan” [loved have] used in the TR pattern: “Kepa-k te-a maite d-u.” [Петя-erg чай-sg[abs] любимый[abs.indef] abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]] and 2) and the CV “atsegin izan” [pleasure have], also used in the TR pattern: “Kepa-k te-a atsegin d-u.” [Петя-erg чай-sg[abs] удовольствие[abs.indef] abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]].

Alternative, which is semantically less precised, is the conversive ABS_ERG pattern: “Opari-a-k harri-tu d-u Kepa.” [подарок-sg-erg удивлять(ся)-prf abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg] Петя[abs]], which is found with verbs like “txunditu”, “zurtu”. All these verbs are labile. In fact, as experiencer-subject verbs they typically do not take nominal complements and are rather used either as monovalent verbs or with complement clauses.

Same as 122. A near synonym is “samurtu”, mainly used in the French Basque country in the same ABS_COM pattern. Arguably, the ABS_COM pattern has been influenced by the Spanish pattern with “con” and the verb “enfadarse”. Alternative pattern is ABS_DAT [Michelena 1981а: 170].

Same as 117. A near synonym is “samurtu”, mainly used in the French Basque country in the same ABS_COM pattern. Arguably, the ABS_COM pattern has been influenced by the Spanish pattern with “con” and the verb “enfadarse”. Alternative pattern is ABS_DAT [Michelena 1981а: 170].
Near synonyms are “apurtu” and “puskatu”, both used in the TR pattern.

CV. Further alternatives include the same CV used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Kepa-k Miren-i hitz egin d-i-o.” [Петя-erg Маша-dat слово[abs.indf] делать-pfv abs3prs-erg/abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg[erg3sg]] ‘Петя поговорил с Машей’. In French Basque Country, the verb “mintzatu” is used, typically in the ABS_DAT pattern (TR and ERG_DAT are also registered): “Kepa Miren-i mintza-tu zai-o.” [Петя[abs] Маша-dat разговаривать-pfv [abs3prs]abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg] ‘Петя поговорил с Машей’. The distribution of valency patterns mirrors the one observed in Spansih (con) vs. French (à)
CV. Alternative is the CV “alde egin” [side make], also used in the ERG_DAT pattern.

Alternative is the verb “heldu” ‘reach, arrive’. Both verbs can also be used in the ABS_ALLIM pattern: “Kepa itsasertze-raino irits-i da.” [Петя[abs] берег.моря-alllim достичь-pfv abs3sg.prs] ‘Петя достиг берега (моря)’.
Near synonym is the verb “atxiki” used in the same ABS_DAT pattern.

Alternative form of the verb is “itxoin”. Apart from that, the CV “zain egon” [waiter[abs.indf]) be.found] is also commonly used, where X is coced by ASB, and Y is coded as a nominal dependent of “zain”: “Kepa Miren-en zain da-go” [Петя[abs] Маша-genp ждущий[abs.indf] abs3prs-находиться[abs.sg]] ‘Петя ждёт Машу’. Firther options are available in the French Basque Country.
Same as 38.

Alternative is the verb “beztitu”, which can be used in either the same TR pattern or (in the dialects) in the “ABS_INS” pattern.

Alternatives are “eraitsi”, “bildu”, used with the same patterns.

comms_internal: Alternative is a construction where X and Y are coordinated, which involves the “lagunak izan” as the predicate: “Kepa eta Miren lagun-ak dira.” [Петя[abs] и Маша[abs] друг-pl[abs] abs3pl.prs] ‘Петя дружит с Машей’ = ‘Петя и Маша – друзья’.

This is the main option in the Spanish Basque country. The same verb is mainly used with the TR pattern in the French Basque country, cf.: “Kepa-k Miren lagun-du d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша[abs] помочь-pfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]] ‘Петя помог Маше’. Interestingly, the transitive pattern has only emerged in the 17th century [EA 1995: 198], arguably due to bother French influence and analogy with other verbs.An additional meaning of the same verbs is ‘accompnay’, with some speakers differentiating between the two meaning by the choice between the TR pattern for accompany and the ERG_DAT pattern for help [Etchebarne 2002: 181].
Near synonyms are “losintxatu”, “balakatu”, and “zurikatu”, all used in the TR pattern. The option chosen is the most frequent one.

This pattern is not frequent. An alternative structure is the one where X and Y are simply coordinated. Alternatively, one can use a variety of periphrastic structures, inclduing liskar egin [argument make], used in the ERG_COM pattern; sesiotan / sesioan / ika-mika [argument go] used in the ABS_COM pattern, etc.

CV. Alternative is the simplex verb “maitatu” also used in the TR pattern: “Kepa-k Miren maita-tzen d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша[abs] любить-ipfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]] ‘Петя любит Машу’. The pattern with the CV “maite izan” is approximately 2,5 times more frequent than the construction with the simplex verb “maitatu”.
A less frequent alternative is “maitemindu”, used in the ABS_COM pattern: “Kepa Miren-ekin maitemin-du da.” [Петя[abs] Маша-com влюбляться-prf abs3sg.prs] ‘Петя влюбился в Машу’.

comms_internal: CV. There are three alternatives to this structure. First, almost identical construction with eduki ‘иметь, держать’ instead of “izan” and the same structure: “Kepa-k buru-ko min-a dauka” [Петя-erg голова-genloc боль-sg[abs] abs3sg.prs.иметь.erg3sg] ‘У Пети болит голова’. Second, the same predicate “mina izan” can be used in a different pattern: “Kepa-k buru-a-n min d-u.” [Петя-erg голова-sg-in боль[abs.indf] abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]] ‘У Пети болит голова’. Third, there is a construction that is parallel to the very first one, except for a slight difference in the internal shape of the predicate itself “min-ez egon”, “Kepa-k buru-ko min-ez da-go.” [Петя-erg голова-genloc боль-ins.indf abs3prs-находиться[abs.sg]] ‘У Пети болит голова’. All of the alternative construction are less frequent than the very first one.
Near synonyms are “ito” and “sartu”, all used in the same ABS_IN valency pattern.
CV. Alternatives are “pot egin” [kiss make], also used in the ERG_DAT pattern.
A near synonym is “nahastu”. Both verbs can also be sometimes used in the ABS_INS pattern: “Ezti-a esne-a-z nahas-i da.” [мед-sg[abs] молоко-sg-ins смешаться-pfv abs3sg.prs] ‘Мед смешался с молоком’.

Near synonym is “tristatu” (a Spanish loanword), also used in the ABS_MOT pattern. The corresponding TR pattern involving a conversive verb is far more common.

Alternatives are 1) the same verb “pentsatu” with the ABS_IN pattern: “Kepa-k Mirenen-gan pentsa-tzen d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша-in думать-ipfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]]. 2) CVs “gogo-a-n izan” [memory-sg-in have], “gogo-a-n erabili” [memory-sg-in carry], “buru-a-n erabili” [head-sg-in carry], all used with the regular TR pattern: “Kepa-k Miren d-u gogo-a-n” [Петя-erg Маша[abs] abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg] память-sg-in] ‘Петя думает о Маше’. 3) CV “gogoeta egin” [thought[abs.indf] make] used with the erg-ins pattern. 4) CV “gogoeta egin” [thought[abs.indf] make] used in the erg-dat p+ buruz (послелог ‘о’) [EH, тексты]. The verb “pentsatu” is a Spanish loanword, and its ability to be used with the ABS_IN pattern can be related to its origin (cf. the preposition “en” in Spanish). However, the ERG_INS and ERG_DAT are more common patterns in Basque than ERG_IN.
Alternative is the verb “margotu”, also used in the TR pattern.

CV. The valency pattern could have been influenced by Spanish (“estar contento” used in combination with the preposition “con”). Alternative is the non-verbal predicate “kontent izan” [content be], used in the ABS_INS pattern: “Kepa seme-a-z kontent da.” [Петя[abs] сын-sg-ins довольный[abs.indf] abs3sg.prs] ‘Петя доволен своим сыном’. A still further alternative is “poz eman” [joy give] used in the DAT_ERG pattern.

This entry contains a reversed transtive pattern, which is the only regular way to convey the necessary meaning with two overt NPs. “alaitu” is a near synonym used in the same way. A further alternative is “poz hartu” [joy take], but it is normally used as a monovalent predicate (the use of the Y argument in the motivative is commented upon as grammatical but unnatural “eskutzitz-a-gatik письмо-sg-mot”. A still another non-verbal option is to “poz-ik egon” [joy-part be].

This pattern is observed in the Spanish territory. The option available in the French territory involves the TR pattern: “Kepa-k Miren sines-ten d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша[abs] верить-ipfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]] ‘Петя верит Маше’. The ERG_DAT can be partly motivated by etymology: Trask (1997: 88–89) analyzes it ultimately a complex verb: zin ‘truth’ + -etsi ‘consider’. Under this view, the otherwise obligatory ABS slot is occupied by “zin”.
Alternative is the CV “tiro egin” [shot make], used in the same valency pattern.

Probably, a loanword from Spanish “toparse” ‘bump into’. One alternative is the verb “trilatu” used in the same TR pattern. Further alternatives are constructions with CV “topo egin”, “enkontru egin”, “tril egin”, all used in the “ERG_COM” pattern: “Кеpa-k Miren-ekin topo egi-n d-u.” [Петя-erg Маша-com встреча делать-pfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]].

Alternative possibilities are: 1) CV “iseka egin” [mockery make] used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Kepa-k Miren-i iseka egi-ten d-i-o.” [Петя-erg Маша-dat насмешка[abs.indf] делать-ipfv abs3prs-erg/abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg[erg3sg]] and 2) CV “burla egin” [mockery make], also used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Kepak Miren-ez burla egi-ten d-u” [Петя-erg Маша-ins насмешка[abs.indf] делать-ipfv abs3prs[abs.sg]-erg/abs[erg3sg]]. A further alternative is the simplex verb “isekatu”, which is used in some dialects. It is attested with several patterns: “erg_dat”, “abs_dat”, and TR.
Additional synonyms are “aditu”, “konprenitu”. With animate Y arguments, the erg_dat pattern is also possible.

A near synonym is “errukitu”, which is more commonly used in the French Basque country and also typically used in the ABS_INS pattern. Apart from this, both verbs can be used in the ABS_DAT pattern: “Kepa ama-ri urrikal-tzen zai-o.” [Петя[abs] мама[sg]-dat жалеть-imp [abs3prs]abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg]. One more alternative is the CV “pena eman” [grief give], also used in the ERG_DAT pattern: “Ama-k Kepa-ri pena ema-ten d-i-o.” [мать[sg]-erg Петя-dat горе[abs.indef] давать-imp abs3prs-erg/abs/dat[abs.sg]-dat3sg[erg3sg]]. The older pattern of “urrikaldu” was DAT_ABS.

sentence: Kepa-k berun puska bat ur-tu d-u
GLOSSES_EN: PN-ERG свинец кусок один-[ABS.INDF] плавить-PFV ABS3PRS[ABS.SG]-ERG/ABS[ERG3SG]
comms_internal: Further options include verbs like “galdatu”, “funditu”, “urtarazi”, all used in the TR pattern.

comms: Not included in the database because Y is expressed as an NP-internal modifier.
comms_internal: CV. Further alternatives all include the same idea of expressing Y as an adnominal modifier of the noun ‘smell’, coupled with verbs like ‘have’, ‘hold’, ‘give’.

A less frequent alternative involves the verb “irten” [go.out] used in the ABS_ABL pattern: “Kepa jaioterri-tik joan da. (Isabelle: sorterritik joan)” [Петя[abs] родной.(небольшой).город-abl идти[pfv] abs3sg.prs] ‘Петя покинул родной (небольшой) город’. A further alternative involves the CV “alde egin” [side[abs.indf]) make] used in the erg_abl pattern.
Alternative is “gurutzatu” used in the same TR pattern.
Alternatives are “zehatu” and “punitu” (a loanword from Romance languages), both used in the TR pattern.
